functions.author: Kael'Vorr
functions.title: Emberstalker Notes
pages:
Emberstalker Notes

Observations from the ash paths where flame learned patience.

Origin

Emberstalkers came from a realm without sunrise. Heat moved through black dust like weather, and predators learned to follow it.

The Collection

Kael'Vorr watched one stalk a cooling meteor for nine days. He opened a gate before it reached the crater.

In the Overworld

Here, every torch, furnace, and living body is a bright trail. The creature finds this abundance delightful.

Behavior

An Emberstalker is faster than its silhouette suggests. Open ground favors the hunter; barriers and prepared terrain favor you.

Ash

Its Demon Ash is not ordinary soot. It carries a weak memory of the fire that made it.

Heart

A Demon Heart is rare because most infernal bodies burn theirs completely. An intact one can stabilize impossible crafting.

Corrupted XP

Some Emberstalkers carry experience warped by dimensional travel. The substance is valuable at a Demon Altar.

Chaos Keys

A very rare kill may produce a Chaos Key, proof that Kael'Vorr occasionally hides rewards inside the danger itself.

Altar Use

Power a Demon Altar with redstone while a sacrifice stands above it. The altar converts released experience into Corrupted XP Orbs.

Crafting

Ash, hearts, and demon steel form the foundation of Chaos gear. Higher altar tiers reveal stronger recipes.

Field Advice

Do not mistake stillness for confusion. Emberstalkers wait because the warm thing always moves eventually.

Kael'Vorr's Note

A flame that only burns is useful. A flame that hunts is an idea.
